config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I use V-Sync with G-Sync/FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "In NVIDIA Control Panel: Turn ON (acts as frame cap at top of VRR range) In-game: Turn OFF (prevents double-buffering lag) This combination gives you the best of both worlds: tear-free gaming within VRR range and smooth capping at max refresh."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the best response time setting?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Start at Medium/Normal, then test with testufo.com/ghosting. Increase if you see ghosting trails. Decrease if you see inverse ghosting (bright coronas). The \"1ms\" advertised often requires extreme overdrive that causes issues."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I use fullscreen or borderless window?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Competitive games: Exclusive fullscreen (lowest input lag) Casual/multitasking: Borderless windowed (alt-tab faster) Note: Windows 11 optimizations have reduced the difference significantly."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I know if my settings are working?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Use these tests: - Refresh rate: testufo.com (shows actual Hz) - G-Sync/FreeSync: NVIDIA Pendulum or VRR indicator in monitor OSD - Response time: testufo.com/ghosting - Input lag: Feel for responsiveness, or use NVIDIA Reflex"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is HDR worth enabling?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes if: - Your monitor is HDR600 or better - Game has good HDR support - You've calibrated HDR settings No if: - Monitor is only HDR400 - HDR looks washed out - Game has poor HDR implementation ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is HDR worth enabling?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes if: - Your monitor is HDR600 or better - Game has good HDR support - You've calibrated HDR settings No if: - Monitor is only HDR400 - HDR looks washed out - Game has poor HDR implementation ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I know what resolution my monitor supports?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Check your monitor's specifications in the manual or manufacturer website. Most monitors display their native resolution in the product name (e.g., \"24-inch 1080p Monitor\"). You can also check in Windows Display Settings or Mac System Preferences where all supported resolutions are listed in the dropdown menu."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between resolution and screen size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Resolution is the number of pixels (e.g., 1920×1080), while screen size is the physical diagonal measurement in inches (e.g., 24\"). The same resolution can appear on different sized screens - a 1080p laptop screen looks much sharper than a 1080p TV because the pixels are packed more densely (higher PPI)."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I use the highest resolution my monitor supports?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generally yes, use your monitor's native resolution for the sharpest image. However, consider performance: higher resolutions require more GPU power for gaming and may make text/UI elements very small, requiring scaling adjustments for comfortable viewing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Why does my 4K monitor look blurry on Windows?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "This is usually due to scaling issues. 4K monitors at 100% scaling make everything tiny, so Windows automatically scales to 150-200%. Some older applications don't handle scaling well. Try adjusting scaling in Display Settings or enable \"Fix scaling for apps\" in Windows 10/11."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use different resolutions on multiple monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, modern operating systems handle different resolutions well. However, mouse movement between screens might feel inconsistent, and window dragging can be awkward if resolutions differ significantly. For best experience, try to match resolutions or at least aspect ratios."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What resolution should I use for streaming or recording?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For streaming: 1080p 60fps is the most common. Use 720p 60fps for slower internet or 1440p if you have excellent upload speed. For recording: Use your native resolution for best quality, or 1080p for broader compatibility and smaller file sizes."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I change my resolution if I can't see the screen properly?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Boot into Safe Mode (Windows) or Recovery Mode (Mac) where lower resolutions are used by default. On Windows, you can also press Windows + P to cycle through display modes, or use Windows + I to open Settings and navigate to Display by keyboard. On Mac, reset NVRAM/PRAM by holding Option + Command + P + R during startup."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is higher resolution always better?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Not necessarily. Higher resolution provides more detail and screen space but requires more GPU power, makes text smaller (requiring scaling), and creates larger file sizes for content creation. The \"best\" resolution depends on your screen size, use case, graphics hardware, and viewing distance. ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is higher resolution always better?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Not necessarily. Higher resolution provides more detail and screen space but requires more GPU power, makes text smaller (requiring scaling), and creates larger file sizes for content creation. The \"best\" resolution depends on your screen size, use case, graphics hardware, and viewing distance. ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the most common laptop screen size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "15.6 inches is the most common laptop screen size globally, accounting for approximately 40-50% of laptop sales. It offers the best balance between screen real estate, portability, cost, and component availability. The second most popular is 13.3-14 inches for ultraportable laptops."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure a laptop screen with a broken screen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "If the screen is broken but display boundaries are still visible, measure the diagonal of the visible display area (not the bezel). If completely shattered, check your laptop model number on the bottom sticker or search online for your model's specifications. The screen size is almost always listed in official specs."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does laptop screen size include the bezel?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No, laptop screen size does not include the bezel (frame). It only measures the active display area - the diagonal distance of the actual screen. This is why two laptops with the same screen size (e.g., 15.6\") can have different overall dimensions if one has thinner bezels."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I replace my laptop screen with a different size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generally no. Laptop screens are specifically designed for each model. The screen size, mounting points, connector type, and cable length are model-specific. However, you can sometimes find screens with the same physical size but different resolution or panel type (IPS vs. TN) that are compatible. Always verify compatibility before purchasing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between laptop screen size and resolution?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Screen size is the physical diagonal measurement in inches (e.g., 15.6\"). Resolution is the number of pixels (e.g., 1920×1080). They're independent - the same 15.6\" screen can have 1366×768 (HD), 1920×1080 (Full HD), or 3840×2160 (4K) resolution. Higher resolution means sharper image but requires more GPU power."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is a 15.6\" laptop too big to carry?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "It depends on your needs and tolerance. A 15.6\" laptop typically weighs 4-5.5 lbs (1.8-2.5 kg) and fits in most backpacks. For daily commuting or frequent travel, 13-14\" is generally more comfortable. For occasional carry between home and office, 15.6\" is manageable. Consider a lightweight 15.6\" model (under 4 lbs) if you need the screen space but want better portability."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure a MacBook screen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Measure exactly the same way as any laptop: diagonal distance from corner to corner of the active display area only, excluding the black bezel. MacBooks make this easy because the aluminum bezel is clearly distinct from the glass display. For recent models, the size is in the product name (MacBook Pro 14-inch, 16-inch, MacBook Air 13-inch)."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What laptop screen size is best for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "15.6\" to 17.3\" is ideal for gaming laptops. 15.6\" offers the best balance - still somewhat portable, fits gaming hardware, large enough for immersion. 17.3\" provides maximum immersion but sacrifices portability. Screens smaller than 15\" often can't fit high-performance gaming components. Also consider high refresh rate (144Hz+) which is more important than size for competitive gaming. ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What laptop screen size is best for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "15.6\" to 17.3\" is ideal for gaming laptops. 15.6\" offers the best balance - still somewhat portable, fits gaming hardware, large enough for immersion. 17.3\" provides maximum immersion but sacrifices portability. Screens smaller than 15\" often can't fit high-performance gaming components. Also consider high refresh rate (144Hz+) which is more important than size for competitive gaming. ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I measure the screen or the bezel?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Only measure the screen, not the bezel (frame). The advertised monitor size refers only to the visible display area, excluding the plastic or metal frame around it."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is monitor size measured diagonally?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, always diagonally, from one corner of the screen to the diagonally opposite corner. This is the industry standard for all monitors and TVs."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure a curved monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Measure the straight-line diagonal distance, not following the curve. Use a ruler or tape measure and measure as if the screen were flat, from corner to corner in a straight line."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if my measurement is between standard sizes?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Round to the nearest standard size. For example: - 23.5\"-23.9\" = 24\" monitor - 27.5\"-28\" = 27\" monitor - 31.5\"-32.5\" = 32\" monitor Manufacturers sometimes specify 23.8\" or 27.5\", but these are marketed as 24\" and 27\" respectively."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use my phone to measure monitor size?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, if you have a measuring app that uses your phone's camera and AR capabilities. Apps like \"Measure\" (iOS) or \"Measure\" (Android) can estimate dimensions. However, physical measurement is more accurate."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I measure an ultrawide monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Same method: Measure diagonally from corner to corner. Ultrawide monitors have larger diagonal measurements (34\", 38\", 49\") but the measurement technique is identical."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between monitor size and screen resolution?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Monitor size: Physical diagonal measurement (e.g., 27\") Screen resolution: Number of pixels (e.g., 2560×1440) They're independent: - Same size can have different resolutions - Same resolution can appear on different sizes"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How accurate does my measurement need to be?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Within ±0.5 inch is sufficient for most purposes. Manufacturers themselves have small variations (a \"27 inch\" might be 27.0\" or 27.5\"). Round to the nearest standard size for practical purposes. ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How accurate does my measurement need to be?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Within ±0.5 inch is sufficient for most purposes. Manufacturers themselves have small variations (a \"27 inch\" might be 27.0\" or 27.5\"). Round to the nearest standard size for practical purposes. ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What size monitor should I buy for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For most gamers, 27 inches at 1440p is the sweet spot. It offers enough screen real estate for immersion without being too large to see the entire screen comfortably. For competitive esports, some prefer 24 inches at 1080p to see everything at a glance. If you sit further back or want maximum immersion, 32 inches at 4K works well."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 4K worth it in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "It depends on your use case. For content creation, photo editing, and productivity with lots of text, 4K is excellent at 27\" or larger. For gaming, 4K requires a powerful GPU (RTX 4070 or better) to run smoothly at high refresh rates. For casual use and gaming on mid-range hardware, 1440p offers better value."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "IPS or VA for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "IPS is generally better for most gaming due to faster response times and better color accuracy. Choose VA if you primarily play single-player games, watch movies, or game in a dark room where the superior contrast is noticeable. Avoid VA for competitive multiplayer due to potential black smearing."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much should I spend on a monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "A good rule is to spend 15-25% of your total PC budget on the monitor. For a $1000 PC, budget $150-250. For a $2000 PC, budget $300-500. Remember monitors last longer than other components, so it's worth investing in quality."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I need G-Sync or FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "FreeSync is free and works with both AMD and NVIDIA GPUs, making it the practical choice for most buyers. Hardware G-Sync (with the module) offers slightly better performance at the extreme low end of the refresh range, but the premium is only worth it for high-end setups with NVIDIA GPUs."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the best refresh rate for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "144Hz is the sweet spot for most gamers - it's a huge improvement over 60Hz and achievable with mid-range GPUs. 240Hz offers diminishing returns unless you play competitive esports. 60Hz is only acceptable for single-player games or if you have a very weak GPU."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I buy a curved monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Curved monitors are personal preference. They can provide a more immersive experience for gaming and help reduce eye strain on ultrawide displays. However, they're not ideal for professional work requiring straight lines (CAD, graphic design). For standard 16:9 monitors, flat is usually fine."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How long do monitors last?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Quality monitors typically last 5-10 years. LED backlights may dim over time, but modern monitors rarely fail outright. OLED panels may degrade faster due to organic materials, though this takes many years of heavy use. Buy quality and your monitor will outlast several PC upgrades. ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How long do monitors last?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Quality monitors typically last 5-10 years. LED backlights may dim over time, but modern monitors rarely fail outright. OLED panels may degrade faster due to organic materials, though this takes many years of heavy use. Buy quality and your monitor will outlast several PC upgrades. ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Faut-il utiliser V-Sync avec G-Sync/FreeSync ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Dans le panneau NVIDIA : Activez (agit comme plafond d'images en haut de la plage VRR) Dans les jeux : Désactivez (évite le lag du double buffering) Cette combinaison donne le meilleur des deux mondes : pas de tearing dans la plage VRR et un plafonnement fluide au taux max."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Quel réglage de temps de réponse choisir ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Commencez à Moyen/Normal, puis testez avec testufo.com/ghosting. Augmentez si vous voyez des traînées. Diminuez si vous voyez du ghosting inversé (halos lumineux). Le \"1ms\" annoncé demande souvent un overdrive extrême qui cause des problèmes."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Plein écran ou fenêtré sans bordure ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Jeux compétitifs : Plein écran exclusif (input lag le plus bas) Casual/multitâche : Fenêtré sans bordure (alt-tab plus rapide) Note : Les optimisations de Windows 11 ont beaucoup réduit la différence."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Le HDR vaut-il le coup ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui si : - Votre écran est HDR600 ou mieux - Le jeu a un bon support HDR - Vous avez calibré les réglages HDR Non si : - L'écran est seulement HDR400 - Le HDR paraît délavé - Le jeu a une mauvaise implémentation HDR ---"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Le HDR vaut-il le coup ?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Oui si : - Votre écran est HDR600 ou mieux - Le jeu a un bon support HDR - Vous avez calibré les réglages HDR Non si : - L'écran est seulement HDR400 - Le HDR paraît délavé - Le jeu a une mauvaise implémentation HDR ---"
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 1440p worth it over 1080p for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, if you have at least an RTX 4060 Ti or RX 7700 XT and primarily play AAA games. The 33% increase in sharpness on a 27\" monitor is significant, and modern GPUs can maintain 100+ FPS in most titles."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much FPS do you lose going from 1080p to 1440p?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Approximately 40 50% FPS loss at 1440p compared to 1080p on the same GPU and settings. A game running at 200 FPS at 1080p will run around 110 120 FPS at 1440p."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can you tell the difference between 1080p and 1440p?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, definitely —especially on 27\" monitors. Text, UI elements, and distant details are noticeably sharper at 1440p. On 24\" monitors, the difference is less pronounced but still visible."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What GPU do I need for 1440p 144Hz gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Minimum : RTX 4060 Ti or RX 7700 XT (100 120 FPS in most games) Recommended : RTX 4070 or RX 7800 XT (120 144 FPS in most games) Ideal : RTX 4070 Ti or better (144+ FPS in all games)"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I get 1080p 240Hz or 1440p 144Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "1080p 240Hz : If you play competitive FPS and want maximum performance 1440p 144Hz : If you play a variety of games and value visual quality Budget consideration : They cost about the same (~$1,500 1,800 total build), so choose based on gaming preference, not budget."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 1080p still good in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Absolutely yes . 1080p remains the competitive gaming standard, offers excellent value, and modern games still look great at this resolution with high settings. Over 60% of PC gamers still use 1080p as their primary resolution."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can a GTX 1660 handle 1440p gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For older/less demanding games at medium settings, yes. For modern AAA games, you'll struggle to maintain 60 FPS. A GTX 1660 is best paired with 1080p gaming."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 4K worth it for gaming in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "4K is worth it if you have a high end GPU (RTX 4080+), primarily play single player story games, and use a 32\" or larger monitor. For competitive gaming or budget builds, 1440p offers better value with higher frame rates."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What GPU do I need for 4K gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For smooth 4K 60 FPS gaming: RTX 4070 Ti or RX 7900 XT minimum. For 4K 100+ FPS: RTX 4080 or better. For 4K Ultra 144 FPS: RTX 4090."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can you see the difference between 1440p and 4K?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, but it depends on viewing distance and screen size. On a 27\" monitor at 24 28 inches viewing distance, the difference is noticeable but not dramatic. On 32\"+ displays or when sitting closer, 4K shows clearer advantages."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does 4K gaming cause more input lag?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Slightly. More pixels take longer to render, but with modern GPUs the difference is minimal (1 2ms) and imperceptible to most players. The bigger factor is your target frame rate lower FPS = higher input lag."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I get 1440p 240Hz or 4K 144Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For competitive gaming: 1440p 240Hz. For single player AAA games: 4K 144Hz. For mixed use: 1440p 165Hz offers the best balance."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Will my RTX 4070 handle 4K gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "An RTX 4070 can handle 4K gaming at medium high settings (45 60 FPS) or with DLSS Quality mode. For native 4K Ultra, you'll want an RTX 4080 or better."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much better is 4K than 1440p for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "4K provides ~50% sharper image (more pixels) but requires 2.25× more GPU power. The visual difference is most noticeable in fine details and anti aliasing, but comes at a significant performance cost."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 1440p still good in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Absolutely. 1440p remains the optimal resolution for most PC gamers, offering excellent image quality, high frame rates, and reasonable GPU requirements. It's the recommended resolution for 27\" gaming monitors."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 240Hz worth it over 144Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For serious competitive FPS players, yes—240Hz provides 1.5 2.5% performance improvement and noticeably smoother motion. For most gamers, including competitive enthusiasts, 144Hz offers better value. The upgrade from 144Hz to 240Hz is far less impactful than 60Hz to 144Hz. Consider 165Hz as a middle ground option."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can you see the difference between 144Hz and 240Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most people can see some difference in motion clarity during fast movement, but it's subtle. Studies show 43% of gamers notice improvement in blind testing, compared to 89% who notice 60Hz to 144Hz. Professional FPS players are more sensitive and 76% can identify the difference. Casual gamers often can't tell or don't find it significant."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What GPU do I need for 240Hz gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For competitive FPS (low medium settings): RTX 4060 or better. For AAA games (high settings): RTX 4080 or better. The key is achieving consistent 240+ FPS in your games. Check benchmarks for your specific games and settings. If you can't maintain 240 FPS, you won't fully benefit from 240Hz."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 240Hz better for competitive gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, 240Hz provides a measurable advantage in competitive FPS games. 66% of professional esports players use 240Hz monitors. The improvement includes reduced input lag (2.77ms), better motion clarity, and slight performance gains (1.5 2.5%) in target acquisition. However, skill, game sense, and practice matter far more than refresh rate."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I upgrade from 144Hz to 240Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Only if you're a serious competitive player, have a high end GPU (RTX 4070+), can afford it without sacrifice, and have tested 240Hz and noticed improvement. For most players, the $200 300 premium is better spent on GPU upgrades, better peripherals, or kept for next generation monitor technology."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much better is 240Hz than 165Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "240Hz is 45% faster frame time than 165Hz (4.17ms vs 6.06ms) but the perceptual difference is minimal. Most users cannot tell them apart in blind testing. 165Hz offers excellent value as a middle ground—giving 40% of the benefit of 240Hz over 144Hz for 20% of the cost premium. Unless you're a pro, 165Hz is the smarter choice."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do pro gamers use 240Hz or 360Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most pros use 240Hz (66%), with growing 360Hz adoption (11%) among elite players. 23% still use 144Hz. 240Hz has become the professional standard, offering the best balance of performance, availability, and cost. 360Hz is emerging but remains niche due to high cost and minimal additional benefit."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can 240Hz make you a better gamer?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "240Hz can provide a 1 2% performance advantage in reaction time and tracking accuracy, but it won't make you significantly better. Skill, aim training, game sense, strategy, and practice are far more important. Pro players on 144Hz routinely beat casual players on 240Hz. Think of 240Hz as fine tuning, not a game changer."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Why do pro gamers use small monitors?",

      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is 32 inch too big for FPS gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, 32 inches is generally too large for competitive FPS gaming. Studies show target acquisition is 25 35% slower compared to 24 inch monitors due to excessive eye movement. Only 2% of professional FPS players use 32 inch displays, and those are primarily content creators. 32\" is fine for casual FPS gaming and single player titles."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What monitor size do most FPS pros use?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "78% of professional FPS players use 24 24.5 inch monitors, while 20% use 27 inch displays. The most popular models are BenQ ZOWIE XL2546K and ASUS ROG Swift PG259QN. Larger monitors are extremely rare in competitive play."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I get 1080p or 1440p for FPS gaming?",

        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For 24 inch competitive FPS: 1080p at 240Hz+ is optimal for maximum performance. For 27 inch balanced gaming: 1440p at 165Hz+ offers better visual quality without major performance loss. Most pro players use 1080p for competitive advantage, while enthusiasts prefer 1440p for image quality."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How far should I sit from my gaming monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For 24 inch: 20 24 inches (51 61cm). For 27 inch: 24 28 inches (61 71cm). For 32 inch: 30 36 inches (76 91cm). Sitting closer than optimal causes eye strain; sitting further reduces competitive advantage and immersion."
      }

    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use a TV for FPS gaming?",

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is a curved or flat monitor better for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "It depends on gaming style. Flat monitors are better for competitive gaming (zero distortion, tournament standard, precise aiming), while curved monitors excel at immersive single player gaming (better FOV, reduced eye strain, more cinematic). For most gamers, flat 27\" monitors offer the best versatility."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do curved monitors cause eye strain?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No, curved monitors typically reduce eye strain compared to flat monitors. The curve matches natural eye curvature, reduces eye movement across the screen, and maintains consistent viewing distance. However, some users need 1 2 weeks to adapt to the curvature initially."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Are curved monitors good for competitive gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generally no. 98%+ of professional competitive gamers use flat monitors because curved monitors can introduce subtle geometry distortion that affects aim precision. Tournaments exclusively use flat monitors, so competitive players should train on flat displays for consistency."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What size monitor should be curved vs flat?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27\": Always flat (curvature benefit minimal). 32\": Either works, slight preference curved. 34\" ultrawide: Strongly recommend curved. 49\" super ultrawide: Curved essential. The larger the monitor, the more beneficial curvature becomes."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do curved monitors distort the image?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, slightly. Curved monitors introduce subtle geometric distortion where straight lines may appear slightly curved, especially at screen edges. This is usually not noticeable in games but can affect productivity work, web browsing, and competitive gaming precision."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Are curved monitors worth the extra cost?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Only in specific scenarios: 32\"+ monitors for immersive gaming, ultrawide monitors (essential), sim racing setups. For 24 27\" monitors or competitive gaming, the premium isn't justified. Curved provides real benefits for the right use cases but isn't universally better."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can you wall mount curved monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, but with limitations. Curved monitors don't sit flush against walls and require more depth clearance. Special mounting arms may be needed. Flat monitors are much easier to wall mount and more space efficient."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do streamers prefer curved or flat monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most streamers prefer flat monitors because they record better on camera, provide standard viewer perspective, and offer better versatility for various content types. Curved monitors can create video distortion and are harder to frame properly for streaming setups."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Which is better, G-Sync or FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "FreeSync is better for most gamers in 2025 due to lower cost, broader compatibility, and comparable performance. G Sync only offers advantages in premium segments (G Sync Ultimate) or specific scenarios requiring the absolute best low framerate performance. The value proposition strongly favors FreeSync."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does FreeSync work with NVIDIA GPUs?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, FreeSync works excellently with NVIDIA GPUs through \"G Sync Compatible\" support introduced in 2019. Most FreeSync monitors are certified compatible, providing the same smooth gaming experience as native AMD support. NVIDIA users can choose FreeSync monitors without compatibility concerns."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is G-Sync worth the extra cost?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Rarely in 2025. The $100 500 G Sync premium is only justifiable for enthusiasts seeking G Sync Ultimate features (premium HDR, ULMB) or users who specifically need the best possible low framerate performance. For 90% of gamers, FreeSync Premium provides equivalent experience at much lower cost."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can you use FreeSync with AMD and G-Sync with NVIDIA?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "FreeSync works with both AMD (native) and NVIDIA (G Sync Compatible) GPUs. G Sync only works with NVIDIA GPUs. This makes FreeSync the more flexible choice for users who might change GPU brands in the future."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What VRR range should I look for?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Look for monitors with at least 48 144Hz range with LFC (Low Framerate Compensation). FreeSync Premium certification guarantees this. Avoid basic FreeSync monitors with narrow ranges (e.g., 48 75Hz) as they provide poor low framerate experience."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do consoles support G-Sync or FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "PS5 and Xbox Series X support HDMI 2.1 VRR, which is compatible with FreeSync but not G Sync. If you game on consoles, choose a FreeSync monitor with HDMI 2.1 support. G Sync monitors won't provide VRR functionality with consoles."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I disable VSync when using VRR?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, always disable V Sync when using G Sync or FreeSync. VRR replaces V Sync functionality while providing lower input lag. Enabling both simultaneously can cause conflicts and reduce performance."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does VRR affect image quality?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No, properly implemented VRR doesn't affect static image quality. It only controls the timing of frame delivery to eliminate tearing and stuttering. Colors, sharpness, and other visual aspects remain unchanged."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is a gaming monitor better than a TV for PS5?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For competitive gaming, yes—monitors have lower input lag (1 3ms vs 5 10ms) and faster response times. For single player and immersive gaming, TVs are better—larger screen (55\"+), superior HDR, and more comfortable couch gaming experience. Most PS5 users prefer TVs for the living room setup, but competitive players choose monitors."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use a TV as a PC monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, but with considerations. Modern 4K TVs work well as PC monitors for productivity and gaming. However: (1) TVs are large (43\"+) requiring more desk space, (2) text clarity may be lower than native monitors at close viewing, (3) some TVs have chroma subsampling issues in PC mode. Best for couch gaming, not ideal for desk work."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What size monitor is best for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24\" for competitive esports (1080p 144 240Hz), 27\" for balanced gaming (1440p 144 165Hz), 32\" for immersive gaming (1440p/4K). Larger than 32\" becomes unwieldy for desk use. Professional gamers prefer 24 27\" for better visual tracking of entire screen without head movement."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do gaming TVs have higher input lag than monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, but the gap has narrowed. Best gaming monitors: 1 3ms input lag. Best gaming TVs: 5 10ms input lag. The 4 7ms difference matters in competitive esports but is negligible in casual gaming. Budget TVs (30ms+) still have poor input lag—avoid for gaming."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is OLED or QLED better for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "OLED is better for image quality (perfect blacks, infinite contrast, instant response time) but has burn in risk and costs more. QLED is brighter (better for bright rooms), no burn in risk, and usually cheaper. For gaming: OLED for best experience, QLED for better value and peace of mind. Both are good choices in 2025."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can a 60Hz TV be good for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For single player and casual gaming, yes—60Hz is adequate. For competitive gaming, no—60Hz feels sluggish compared to 120Hz+ after acclimation. PS5 and Xbox Series X support 120Hz in many games, making 60Hz TVs a bottleneck. Minimum recommendation: 120Hz for modern console gaming."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Why do pros use gaming monitors instead of TVs?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Four reasons: (1) Lower input lag (1 3ms vs 5 10ms) for faster reactions, (2) Higher refresh rates (240 360Hz vs max 120Hz TV), (3) Smaller size (24 27\") allows tracking entire screen without eye movement, (4) Desk setup is tournament standard. No competitive disadvantages tolerated at pro level."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is a curved monitor or TV better for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Curved monitors (1800R 1000R) provide more immersive experience for desk gaming, reduce eye strain, and improve viewing angles. Most beneficial at 27\"+ and ultrawide formats. Curved TVs are rare and unnecessary—viewing distance too great for curve to matter. For desk gaming: curved is nice bonus. For living room: flat is fine."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is HDR worth it for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, but only with proper HDR monitors (HDR1000+ or OLED). True HDR gaming provides significantly better contrast, more realistic lighting, and enhanced immersion in supported games. However, avoid fake HDR (HDR400/600) monitors as they provide minimal benefit over good SDR displays."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What's the difference between HDR400 and HDR1000?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "HDR400 is \"fake HDR\" with insufficient brightness (400 nits) and no local dimming, providing minimal improvement over SDR. HDR1000 requires 1000 nits peak brightness and local dimming (≥384 zones), delivering true HDR experience with deeper blacks and brighter highlights simultaneously."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I need a special GPU for HDR gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most modern GPUs support HDR (GTX 1000+ series, RX 500+ series). However, HDR gaming requires ~10 15% more GPU power due to increased bandwidth and processing requirements. For 1440p HDR gaming, RTX 3060+ or RX 6600 XT+ recommended."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use HDR for competitive gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "HDR can work for competitive gaming, but many pros avoid it due to potential input lag, inconsistent brightness, and non standard appearance. If using HDR competitively, ensure your monitor has <5ms input lag in HDR mode and practice extensively with HDR enabled."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I buy OLED or Mini-LED for HDR gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "OLED provides better HDR experience (perfect blacks, infinite contrast) but has burn in risk and costs more. Mini LED offers good HDR with no burn in risk and better brightness, but with some blooming. For mixed gaming/productivity: Mini LED. For pure gaming experience: OLED."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Why does HDR look washed out in some games?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Poor HDR implementation, incorrect monitor settings, or fake HDR monitors cause washed out appearance. Ensure you have true HDR monitor (HDR1000+), configure peak brightness correctly in game, and use monitors with proper local dimming. Some games have poor HDR implementation regardless of hardware."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is Windows HDR mode necessary for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, Windows HDR mode must be enabled for HDR gaming, but it often makes desktop content look washed out. Best practice: enable HDR only when gaming, disable for regular desktop use. Windows 11 has improved HDR handling compared to Windows 10."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much should I spend on an HDR gaming monitor?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For true HDR gaming: minimum $800 1000 (entry OLED or basic Mini LED). Budget $1200 2000 for excellent HDR experience. Below $800, focus on high quality SDR monitor instead of fake HDR. Don't pay premium for HDR400/600 certification."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is IPS or TN better for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "IPS is better for most gamers in 2025. Modern Fast IPS offers 1 2ms response times (nearly matching TN) with vastly superior color accuracy and viewing angles. TN is only better if you need 360Hz+ on a strict budget. 59% of professional gamers now use IPS vs 36% TN."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the best panel type for competitive gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Fast IPS (1 2ms G2G) at 240Hz+ is optimal for competitive gaming in 2025. It balances TN like speed with better colors and no viewing angle issues. OLED is even better but expensive. TN remains viable on budget but image quality suffers significantly."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is VA good for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "VA is good for single player immersive gaming (horror, RPGs, space sims) where contrast matters more than speed. VA is poor for competitive or fast paced gaming due to slow response times (4 8ms) and black smearing. Not recommended for FPS, racing, or action games."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do I need OLED for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No, OLED is not needed but offers the ultimate gaming experience with perfect blacks, instant response, and vibrant colors. IPS provides 85 90% of OLED quality at half the price without burn in concerns. OLED is worth it for enthusiasts who can afford $1000+ and will use varied content to avoid burn in."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is IPS glow and is it a problem?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "IPS glow is light leakage visible in screen corners when viewing dark content in dark rooms. It varies by unit quality and viewing angle. Modern IPS has reduced this issue significantly. It's noticeable but usually minor annoyance, not a deal breaker for most users. OLED eliminates it completely."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can you see the difference between 1ms and 4ms response time?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most gamers cannot clearly distinguish 1ms from 4ms response time in typical gaming. The difference becomes noticeable above 5 6ms as motion blur increases. Professional competitive players may notice 2 3ms differences. For most gamers, 1 4ms is \"fast enough\" if other monitor qualities (color, refresh rate) are good."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is TN dead for gaming monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Not dead but declining. TN retains advantages in extreme budget 240Hz+ monitors and 360 500Hz esports displays. However, Fast IPS has largely replaced TN in mainstream gaming. Major manufacturers are reducing TN models. By 2026 2027, TN will likely be niche/legacy technology except at extreme refresh rates."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What panel does my favorite streamer use?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most popular streamers use IPS or OLED panels because they look better on camera and provide better image quality for viewers. While some competitive streamers use TN for performance, IPS is overwhelmingly preferred for streaming due to superior color reproduction that translates better to video encoding."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Is ultrawide better than dual monitors for gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For immersive single player gaming, yes—ultrawide provides seamless, cinematic experience without bezels. For competitive gaming and multitasking, dual monitors are better due to 16:9 compatibility, secondary screen for Discord/guides, and higher refresh rate availability. 85% of games support ultrawide, but competitive titles often don't."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use an ultrawide monitor for competitive FPS games?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Most competitive FPS games either don't support ultrawide (CS2, Valorant) or the extra FOV is considered a disadvantage. 99% of professional players use standard 16:9 monitors. Ultrawides are tournament banned. For casual competitive play, some games like COD and Apex work, but you may be at a disadvantage."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What GPU do I need for ultrawide gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "For 3440×1440 (21:9) at 60 FPS: RTX 4060 Ti minimum. For 144 FPS: RTX 4070 or better. For 5120×1440 (32:9) at 60 FPS: RTX 4070. For 144 FPS: RTX 4080 or 4090. Ultrawide requires 34 100% more GPU power than standard 1440p."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Are ultrawide monitors worth it in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, if you primarily play single player games, do content creation, or value immersive experiences. Not worth it if you play competitive FPS, stream regularly, or need true multitasking. Consider your primary use case—ultrawides excel at specific tasks but aren't universally better."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I get a 34\" ultrawide or dual 27\" monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "34\" ultrawide if: immersive gaming, video editing, programming, clean aesthetics. Dual 27\" if: competitive gaming, streaming, multitasking, flexibility. Budget: dual 27\" can be cheaper ($700 vs $500 800). Performance: ultrawide needs better GPU."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do all games support ultrawide monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "No. About 85% of modern AAA games fully support 21:9. Most competitive FPS don't (CS2, Valorant locked to 16:9). 32:9 support is ~45%. Older games and some console ports lack support. Community mods exist but aren't recommended for online games."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use a 49\" ultrawide as two monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes, most 49\" ultrawide monitors have Picture by Picture (PbP) mode that treats them as two separate displays. However, you'll have a bezel free split in the middle. Better than physical bezel but not true dual monitors. Windows treats it as one or two displays depending on mode."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Which is better for productivity: ultrawide or dual monitors?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Depends on workflow. Ultrawide better for: coding, video editing, seamless workflows, clean aesthetics. Dual monitors better for: video calls + work, completely separate applications, mixed orientations (landscape + portrait), window management. Most productive: 49\" ultrawide or triple monitors, but overkill for many."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Lohnt sich 1440p gegenüber 1080p für Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, wenn du mindestens eine RTX 4060 Ti oder RX 7700 XT hast und hauptsächlich AAA Spiele spielst. Die 33% Verbesserung der Schärfe auf einem 27\" Monitor ist signifikant, und moderne GPUs können in den meisten Spielen 100+ FPS bei 1440p halten."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Wie viel FPS verliert man beim Wechsel von 1080p zu 1440p?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ungefähr 40 50% FPS Verlust bei 1440p im Vergleich zu 1080p auf derselben GPU und denselben Einstellungen. Ein Spiel, das bei 1080p mit 200 FPS läuft, läuft bei 1440p mit etwa 110 120 FPS."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Kann man den Unterschied zwischen 1080p und 1440p sehen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, definitiv – besonders auf 27\" Monitoren. Text, UI Elemente und entfernte Details sind bei 1440p merklich schärfer. Auf 24\" Monitoren ist der Unterschied weniger ausgeprägt, aber immer noch sichtbar."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche GPU brauche ich für 1440p 144Hz Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Minimum : RTX 4060 Ti oder RX 7700 XT (100 120 FPS in den meisten Spielen) Empfohlen : RTX 4070 oder RX 7800 XT (120 144 FPS in den meisten Spielen) Ideal : RTX 4070 Ti oder besser (144+ FPS in allen Spielen)"
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich 1080p 240Hz oder 1440p 144Hz nehmen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "1080p 240Hz : Wenn du kompetitive FPS spielst und maximale Performance willst 1440p 144Hz : Wenn du verschiedene Spiele spielst und visuelle Qualität schätzt Budget Überlegung : Sie kosten etwa gleich viel (~1.500 1.800€ Gesamtbuild), also wähle basierend auf Gaming Präferenz, nicht Budget."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist 1080p 2025 noch gut?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Absolut ja . 1080p bleibt der kompetitive Gaming Standard, bietet exzellentes Preis Leistungs Verhältnis, und moderne Spiele sehen bei dieser Auflösung mit hohen Einstellungen immer noch großartig aus. Über 60% der PC Gamer nutzen 1080p immer noch als primäre Auflösung."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Lohnt sich 4K Gaming in 2025?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "4K lohnt sich, wenn Sie eine High End GPU (RTX 4080+) haben, hauptsächlich Single Player Story Spiele spielen und einen 32\" oder größeren Monitor verwenden. Für kompetitives Gaming oder Budget Builds bietet 1440p besseren Wert mit höheren Bildraten."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche GPU brauche ich für 4K Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für flüssiges 4K 60 FPS Gaming: RTX 4070 Ti oder RX 7900 XT minimum. Für 4K 100+ FPS: RTX 4080 oder besser. Für 4K Ultra 144 FPS: RTX 4090."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Kann man den Unterschied zwischen 1440p und 4K sehen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, aber es hängt von Betrachtungsabstand und Bildschirmgröße ab. Bei einem 27\" Monitor mit 60 70 cm Betrachtungsabstand ist der Unterschied bemerkbar, aber nicht dramatisch. Bei 32\"+ Displays oder näherem Sitzen zeigt 4K klarere Vorteile."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Verursacht 4K Gaming mehr Input-Lag?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Geringfügig. Mehr Pixel brauchen länger zum Rendern, aber mit modernen GPUs ist der Unterschied minimal (1 2ms) und für die meisten Spieler nicht wahrnehmbar. Der größere Faktor ist Ihre Ziel Bildrate niedrigere FPS = höherer Input Lag."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich 1440p 240Hz oder 4K 144Hz nehmen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für kompetitives Gaming: 1440p 240Hz. Für Single Player AAA Spiele: 4K 144Hz. Für gemischte Nutzung: 1440p 165Hz bietet die beste Balance."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Wird meine RTX 4070 4K Gaming bewältigen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Eine RTX 4070 kann 4K Gaming bei mittleren hohen Einstellungen (45 60 FPS) oder mit DLSS Qualitätsmodus bewältigen. Für natives 4K Ultra möchten Sie eine RTX 4080 oder besser."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist 1440p in 2025 noch gut?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Absolut. 1440p bleibt die optimale Auflösung für die meisten PC Gamer und bietet hervorragende Bildqualität, hohe Bildraten und angemessene GPU Anforderungen. Es ist die empfohlene Auflösung für 27\" Gaming Monitore."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Lohnt sich 240Hz über 144Hz?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für ernsthafte kompetitive FPS Spieler, ja—240Hz bietet 1,5 2,5% Leistungsverbesserung und spürbar geschmeidigere Bewegung. Für die meisten Gamer, einschließlich kompetitiver Enthusiasten, bietet 144Hz besseren Wert. Das Upgrade von 144Hz auf 240Hz ist weit weniger impaktvoll als 60Hz auf 144Hz. Betrachten Sie 165Hz als Mittelweg Option."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Können Sie den Unterschied zwischen 144Hz und 240Hz sehen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Die meisten Leute können etwas Unterschied in Bewegungsklarheit während schneller Bewegung sehen, aber es ist subtil. Studien zeigen, dass 43% der Gamer Verbesserung in Blindtests bemerken, verglichen mit 89%, die 60Hz zu 144Hz bemerken. Professionelle FPS Spieler sind empfindlicher und 76% können den Unterschied identifizieren. Casual Gamer können oft nicht sagen oder finden es nicht signifikant."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche GPU brauche ich für 240Hz Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für kompetitive FPS (niedrig mittlere Einstellungen): RTX 4060 oder besser. Für AAA Spiele (hohe Einstellungen): RTX 4080 oder besser. Der Schlüssel ist, konsistente 240+ FPS in Ihren Spielen zu erreichen. Überprüfen Sie Benchmarks für Ihre spezifischen Spiele und Einstellungen. Wenn Sie 240 FPS nicht aufrechterhalten können, werden Sie nicht vollständig von 240Hz profitieren."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist 240Hz besser für kompetitives Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, 240Hz bietet einen messbaren Vorteil in kompetitiven FPS Spielen. 66% der professionellen Esports Spieler verwenden 240Hz Monitore. Die Verbesserung umfasst reduzierten Input Lag (2,77ms), bessere Bewegungsklarheit und leichte Leistungsgewinne (1,5 2,5%) in Zielerfassung. Jedoch sind Skill, Game Sense und Übung weit wichtiger als Bildwiederholrate."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich von 144Hz auf 240Hz upgraden?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Nur wenn Sie ein ernsthafter kompetitiver Spieler sind, eine High End GPU (RTX 4070+) haben, es sich ohne Opfer leisten können und 240Hz getestet und Verbesserung bemerkt haben. Für die meisten Spieler ist die €200 300 Prämie besser in GPU Upgrades, bessere Peripherie ausgegeben oder für nächste Generation Monitor Technologie aufbewahrt."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Kann 240Hz Sie zu einem besseren Gamer machen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "240Hz kann einen 1 2% Leistungsvorteil in Reaktionszeit und Tracking Genauigkeit bieten, aber es wird Sie nicht signifikant besser machen. Skill, Zieltraining, Game Sense, Strategie und Übung sind weit wichtiger. Pro Spieler auf 144Hz schlagen routinemäßig Casual Spieler auf 240Hz. Denken Sie an 240Hz als Feintuning, nicht als Game Changer."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist 24 oder 27 Zoll besser für FPS?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 Zoll ist besser für rein kompetitives FPS Gaming (CS2, Valorant) aufgrund schnellerer Zielerfassung und vollständigem peripherem Sichtfeld. 27 Zoll ist besser für ausgewogenes Gaming, das kompetitive und Casual FPS Titel mischt, und bietet bessere Immersion bei gleichzeitiger Wettbewerbsfähigkeit. 78% der FPS Profis verwenden 24 Zoll Monitore."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Warum verwenden Pro-Gamer kleine Monitore?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pro Gamer verwenden 24 Zoll Monitore, weil sie den gesamten Bildschirm ohne Augen oder Kopfbewegung sehen können, was zu 15 25ms schnelleren Reaktionszeiten führt. Kleinere Bildschirme ermöglichen besseres peripheres Bewusstsein, schnellere Informationsverarbeitung und sind der Turnier Standard in den meisten Esports Wettbewerben."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist 32 Zoll zu groß für FPS-Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, 32 Zoll ist generell zu groß für kompetitives FPS Gaming. Studien zeigen, dass die Zielerfassung 25 35% langsamer ist im Vergleich zu 24 Zoll Monitoren aufgrund exzessiver Augenbewegung. Nur 2% der professionellen FPS Spieler verwenden 32 Zoll Displays, und das sind hauptsächlich Content Ersteller. 32\" ist in Ordnung für Casual FPS Gaming und Einzelspieler Titel."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche Monitor-Größe verwenden die meisten FPS-Profis?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "78% der professionellen FPS Spieler verwenden 24 24.5 Zoll Monitore, während 20% 27 Zoll Displays verwenden. Die beliebtesten Modelle sind BenQ ZOWIE XL2546K und ASUS ROG Swift PG259QN. Größere Monitore sind im kompetitiven Spiel extrem selten."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich 1080p oder 1440p für FPS-Gaming nehmen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für 24 Zoll kompetitives FPS: 1080p bei 240Hz+ ist optimal für maximale Performance. Für 27 Zoll ausgewogenes Gaming: 1440p bei 165Hz+ bietet bessere visuelle Qualität ohne großen Performance Verlust. Die meisten Pro Spieler verwenden 1080p für kompetitiven Vorteil, während Enthusiasten 1440p für Bildqualität bevorzugen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Wie weit sollte ich von meinem Gaming-Monitor sitzen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für 24 Zoll: 51 61cm. Für 27 Zoll: 61 71cm. Für 32 Zoll: 76 91cm. Näher als optimal sitzen verursacht Augenbelastung; weiter sitzen reduziert kompetitiven Vorteil und Immersion."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche ist die beste Bildwiederholrate für FPS-Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Minimum: 144Hz (moderner Standard). Empfohlen: 240Hz (kompetitiver Sweet Spot). Professionell: 360Hz+ (Elite Performance). Der Unterschied zwischen 60Hz und 144Hz ist dramatisch; 144Hz zu 240Hz ist bemerkbar; 240Hz zu 360Hz+ ist subtil, kann aber auf professionellem Level wichtig sein."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist ein curved oder flat Monitor besser für Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Es hängt vom Gaming Stil ab. Flat Monitore sind besser für Competitive Gaming (null Verzerrung, Tournament Standard, präzises Zielen), während curved Monitore bei immersivem Einzelspieler Gaming glänzen (besseres FOV, reduzierte Augenbelastung, cinematischer). Für die meisten Gamer bieten flat 27\" Monitore die beste Vielseitigkeit."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Verursachen curved Monitore Augenbelastung?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Nein, curved Monitore reduzieren typischerweise Augenbelastung verglichen mit flat Monitoren. Die Kurve entspricht natürlicher Augenkrümmung, reduziert Augenbewegung über Bildschirm und behält konsistenten Betrachtungsabstand. Jedoch benötigen manche Nutzer 1 2 Wochen um sich an die Krümmung zu gewöhnen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sind curved Monitore gut für Competitive Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Generell nein. 98%+ professioneller Competitive Gamer verwenden flat Monitore, da curved Monitore subtile Geometrie Verzerrung einführen können, die Ziel Präzision beeinflusst. Turniere verwenden ausschließlich flat Monitore, daher sollten competitive Spieler auf flat Displays trainieren für Konsistenz."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche Monitor-Größe sollte curved vs flat sein?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24 27\": Immer flat (Krümmungsvorteil minimal). 32\": Beides funktioniert, leichte Präferenz für curved. 34\" Ultrawide: Stark curved empfohlen. 49\" Super Ultrawide: Curved essentiell. Je größer der Monitor, desto vorteilhafter wird Krümmung."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Verzerren curved Monitore das Bild?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, leicht. Curved Monitore führen subtile geometrische Verzerrung ein, wo gerade Linien leicht gekrümmt erscheinen können, besonders an Bildschirmrändern. Dies ist meist nicht bemerkbar in Spielen, kann aber Produktivitätsarbeit, Web Browsing und Competitive Gaming Präzision beeinträchtigen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sind curved Monitore die zusätzlichen Kosten wert?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Nur in spezifischen Szenarien: 32\"+ Monitore für immersives Gaming, Ultrawide Monitore (essentiell), Sim Racing Setups. Für 24 27\" Monitore oder Competitive Gaming ist der Aufpreis nicht gerechtfertigt. Curved bietet echte Vorteile für richtige Anwendungsfälle, ist aber nicht universell besser."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welches ist besser, G-Sync oder FreeSync?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "FreeSync ist besser für die meisten Gamer in 2025 aufgrund niedrigerer Kosten, breiterer Kompatibilität und vergleichbarer Performance. G Sync bietet nur Vorteile in Premium Segmenten (G Sync Ultimate) oder spezifischen Szenarien, die absolut beste Low Framerate Performance erfordern. Das Wert Angebot bevorzugt stark FreeSync."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Funktioniert FreeSync mit NVIDIA GPUs?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, FreeSync funktioniert exzellent mit NVIDIA GPUs durch \"G Sync Compatible\" Support, eingeführt 2019. Die meisten FreeSync Monitore sind kompatibel zertifiziert und bieten dieselbe smooth Gaming Erfahrung wie native AMD Unterstützung. NVIDIA Nutzer können FreeSync Monitore ohne Kompatibilitäts Bedenken wählen."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist G-Sync die zusätzlichen Kosten wert?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Selten in 2025. Der €100 500 G Sync Aufschlag ist nur für Enthusiasten rechtfertigbar, die G Sync Ultimate Features (Premium HDR, ULMB) suchen oder Nutzer, die speziell die bestmögliche Low Framerate Performance benötigen. Für 90% der Gamer bietet FreeSync Premium äquivalente Erfahrung zu viel niedrigeren Kosten."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Funktioniert FreeSync mit AMD und G-Sync mit NVIDIA?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "FreeSync funktioniert mit sowohl AMD (nativ) als auch NVIDIA (G Sync Compatible) GPUs. G Sync funktioniert nur mit NVIDIA GPUs. Das macht FreeSync zur flexibleren Wahl für Nutzer, die in Zukunft GPU Marken wechseln könnten."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welchen VRR-Bereich sollte ich suchen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Suchen Sie nach Monitoren mit mindestens 48 144Hz Bereich mit LFC (Low Framerate Compensation). FreeSync Premium Zertifizierung garantiert dies. Vermeiden Sie Basic FreeSync Monitore mit engen Bereichen (z.B. 48 75Hz), da sie schlechte Low Framerate Erfahrung bieten."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

structured_data.keywords: Gaming Monitor oder TV, Monitor oder Fernseher zum Zocken, TV als Gaming Monitor, OLED TV Gaming, Gaming Fernseher Test, Monitor für Konsole, PS5 Monitor oder TV, Gaming Display Vergleich

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist Gaming-Monitor oder TV besser für PS5?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Für kompetitives Gaming ja—Monitore haben niedrigeren Input Lag (1 3ms vs 5 10ms) und schnellere Reaktionszeiten. Für Einzelspieler und immersives Gaming sind TVs besser—größerer Bildschirm (55\"+), überlegenes HDR und komfortableres Couch Gaming Erlebnis. Meiste PS5 Nutzer bevorzugen TVs für Wohnzimmer Setup, aber kompetitive Spieler wählen Monitore."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Kann ich TV als PC-Monitor nutzen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, aber mit Überlegungen. Moderne 4K TVs funktionieren gut als PC Monitore für Produktivität und Gaming. Jedoch: (1) TVs sind groß (43\"+) benötigen mehr Schreibtischplatz, (2) Textklarheit kann niedriger sein als native Monitore bei nahem Betrachten, (3) manche TVs haben Chroma Subsampling Probleme im PC Modus. Am besten für Couch Gaming, nicht ideal für Schreibtischarbeit."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Welche Monitor-Größe ist am besten für Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "24\" für kompetitive Esports (1080p 144 240Hz), 27\" für ausgewogenes Gaming (1440p 144 165Hz), 32\" für immersives Gaming (1440p/4K). Größer als 32\" wird unhandlich für Schreibtisch Nutzung. Professionelle Gamer bevorzugen 24 27\" für besseres visuelles Tracking des gesamten Bildschirms ohne Kopfbewegung."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Haben Gaming-TVs höheren Input-Lag als Monitore?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, aber die Lücke hat sich verengt. Beste Gaming Monitore: 1 3ms Input Lag. Beste Gaming TVs: 5 10ms Input Lag. Der 4 7ms Unterschied zählt in kompetitivem Esports aber ist vernachlässigbar in Casual Gaming. Budget TVs (30ms+) haben weiterhin schlechten Input Lag—vermeiden für Gaming."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist OLED oder QLED besser für Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "OLED ist besser für Bildqualität (perfekte Schwarzwerte, unendlicher Kontrast, sofortige Reaktionszeit) aber hat Burn In Risiko und kostet mehr. QLED ist heller (besser für helle Räume), kein Burn In Risiko und meist günstiger. Für Gaming: OLED für bestes Erlebnis, QLED für besseren Wert und Seelenfrieden. Beide sind gute Wahlen in 2025."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>

config.faq_structured_data:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Ist HDR für Gaming wert?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Ja, aber nur mit ordentlichen HDR Monitoren (HDR1000+ oder OLED). Echtes HDR Gaming bietet signifikant besseren Kontrast, realistischere Beleuchtung und verstärkte Immersion in unterstützten Spielen. Jedoch vermeiden Sie Fake HDR (HDR400/600) Monitore, da sie minimalen Vorteil gegenüber guten SDR Displays bieten."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Was ist der Unterschied zwischen HDR400 und HDR1000?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "HDR400 ist \"Fake HDR\" mit unzureichender Helligkeit (400 Nits) und ohne Local Dimming, bietet minimale Verbesserung gegenüber SDR. HDR1000 erfordert 1000 Nits Peak Helligkeit und Local Dimming (≥384 Zonen), liefert echte HDR Erfahrung mit tieferen Schwarzwerten und helleren Highlights gleichzeitig."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Brauche ich eine spezielle GPU für HDR-Gaming?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Die meisten modernen GPUs unterstützen HDR (GTX 1000+ Serie, RX 500+ Serie). Jedoch erfordert HDR Gaming ~10 15% mehr GPU Power aufgrund erhöhter Bandbreiten und Verarbeitungsanforderungen. Für 1440p HDR Gaming empfohlen RTX 3060+ oder RX 6600 XT+."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Sollte ich OLED oder Mini-LED für HDR-Gaming kaufen?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "OLED bietet bessere HDR Erfahrung (perfekte Schwarzwerte, unendlicher Kontrast) hat aber Burn In Risiko und kostet mehr. Mini LED bietet gutes HDR ohne Burn In Risiko und bessere Helligkeit, aber mit etwas Blooming. Für gemischtes Gaming/Produktivität: Mini LED. Für pure Gaming Erfahrung: OLED."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Warum sieht HDR in manchen Spielen verwaschen aus?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Schlechte HDR Implementierung, falsche Monitor Einstellungen oder Fake HDR Monitore verursachen verwaschenes Aussehen. Stellen Sie sicher, dass Sie einen echten HDR Monitor (HDR1000+) haben, Peak Helligkeit im Spiel korrekt konfigurieren und Monitore mit ordentlichem Local Dimming verwenden. Manche Spiele haben schlechte HDR Implementierung unabhängig von Hardware."
      }
    }
  ]
}
</script>
